textByHash.0a9c0006c0580dd4a67edb18508b95abe96b4935: Besides, if I leave this place then I won't be able to help any other adventurers with their banking needs.
textByHash.1f1289d1ad10af4f286ac6821435591871a6a9ad: What do you take me for? Some kind of pack yak?
textByHash.1f6372e9a616bdb0e0d0573c1558e659021d29e0: Every adventurer seems to destroy without any thought as to what they are doing, nor with any intent to restore what was lost.
textByHash.24fe9081de578c96de01367f2120ba1e423c3db9: It's a shame that much of the damage cannot truly be undone, but I know that Het, Apmeken, Crondis and even Scabaras will be grateful for your actions.
textByHash.28cfaefc9b7e5bd7aba392cb6ab37e84c314a808: Not particularly. We camels are known for our durability in harsh environments. I think you should be more concerned about yourself. You humans seem so much more fragile than many other species.
textByHash.2c3ff58abc1c75bf78d4786ee83751d2ef23ea7a: You may say that you are here to fight the corruption. Indeed, there may be some truth to that. However, would you really be here if there wasn't something for you to gain?
textByHash.2cf92612e95dac6ead5a07387759244ff99343c6: Now, I have something for you. I've been looking for someone worthy enough to pass this on to.
textByHash.30c646b14095fa99892fc7743ff32004c047842f: Well there are ghost bankers, gnome bankers, dwarf bankers, goblin bankers and more! Is it really all that surprising that camels can do it too?
textByHash.330363ce67c231a60f71430b3cdda235ecf21607: Why do you care what opinions a camel has of you?
textByHash.33e70093a771f5b7a10a079c2c37eb166acbd7ab: If you can do that, then that would prove to me that you are infact different from the rest.
textByHash.3be288540ddb9219622be02758a14e19d2d6c82d: That's for you to figure out.
textByHash.3d3211a552aae43a801c1439bcbc39a0dade4eb3: I won't be eating this, but I do like to collect them. Thank you.
textByHash.450398b0c8638ea6953f28e9dd8a86afc528835c: Well, it really was inevitable, which is a shame really. It saddens me to see what has happened to this tomb.
textByHash.46320ad5a8a03b58f006962a439ddba690994cb7: Ah, the ashes of Akkha, a great warrior. It's a shame that his soul never got to experience the release of death.
textByHash.4b7ce78fff1abfe64fdb6a9084e71e251500223b: Don't you understand how banking works? I don't have all of your stuff, I have simply been trained to access the place where it is all stored.
textByHash.4c7221e385633764063a3dfa0819a0b8235b1b99: And it conveniently found its way back to me. Try not to lose it this time.
textByHash.4c8bf5dbfe949ecd2a8dfabccec9091cf14ab9b7: Oh, never mind, I was just thinking about something.
textByHash.519d40993131f6aa0419a169061da7915aafbc15: This is why I eat the grain in that sack over there. It's specially grown out here in the desert. Once harvested, it goes through a preparation process of several years that...
textByHash.523de909aa9e6ab6c64279016b26abd17a9daa93: We? Are you sure about that? I find you adventurers often have ulterior motives.
textByHash.543b8903333dc92038f0a584ff648a0e82d415c5: But you don't even use a bank.
textByHash.56728ec2ee62c4bd9124145c828075387cdc1214: ...and that's why this grain is so special. Not to mention so hard to come by.
textByHash.68b7e3e2a5278ee23d4e13788384378353882839: So you only care because my words cut close to the bone? If you truly want to prove me wrong, then it's your outlook that should change.
textByHash.74cf43ae3cfb392f131c78224e76b1583fbb303a: Well I hope you're very proud of yourself. Your arbitrary restrictions don't make you better than everyone else, you know.
textByHash.8f8b2aa9be933c4cdae0d8361b7c14960458905a: Well well, colour me impressed adventurer. It seems that you do care after all.
textByHash.965e0b9047d3bcddc71165ac6ade1423ebf91e4e: Hey, get your hands off my food! Unless you'd like me to start eating the contents of your bank instead!
textByHash.a7f781c93b9dad61c57c6591e1edd64eb3f9e84e: Well, it does take a special kind of camel to master the portal magic used for bank access.
textByHash.af20a45126ddeb18764644605729cd5ae9a5f0a2: Faeces! How lovely. If I'm not mistaken, this dung is from Kephri herself, last of the dawn scarabs. It's a shame that her kind have long been lost to the world.
textByHash.aff9bf12f48b4c706f134c498726ecb5e55e1f45: My, this is some good grain, adventurer! Great flavour profile, not too salty and with just a hint of sweetness. It almost reminds me of...
textByHash.b08ff0de4ee5684f30229d84198580ecb1bb8bcd: If your only goal in life is to be liked by all, then you'll probably spend the rest of it chasing the impossible.
textByHash.c9699a28eb3d988866f8a03450a4b855f9944cdf: Ah, the fang of the mighty Zebak. What a cruel fate to be consumed by ever-lasting hunger.
textByHash.cd8cf1a84d75ad16e26740db55d456efd6e9191c: Quite the banana you have there! Ba-Ba and her baboon troop were always known for finding the biggest of these. It's a shame that their memory will now disappear into history.
textByHash.d130f71afbf3673f92f1b996c597a30e20984e83: Why would I want to do that?
textByHash.dcacd78dc6272cd470fa196a0e6162658c3a1331: What does it look like I'm doing? I'm here to help cleanse the Devourer's corruption from this sacred place.
textByHash.e211ffaea3a6d6f06260c55f6a6a7bb29b701679: I don't have any of your stuff.
textByHash.eede35f1640aa6812f70ea5db487058f13163568: I'll leave that as another puzzle for you to solve.
textByHash.f6e2114d2ef1ad320154f4143977c1672e707f56: Bleh, this grain is disgusting! Do you think I am but a simple cow, adventurer?
textByHash.f885093f2a996e77ed91e8bd3dcbca0788b459e8: I hear you speak those words, but I feel no conviction behind them.
textByHash.fa2ce091aed02033f6ee783fca422261e5aa9356: My name is my own, adventurer. It's hardly important right now.
textByHash.fca9e55d410c5bbaf1f59e7f6c0967b5e22639c9: Well, just try not to desecrate this place any more than it already has been.
textByHash.ffea0c1ef9630df52c8f62d5ece70e3f5421b85e: We always do, but you have still done more than most.
